report_meta.title: Panelin GPT — API-Centric Architecture Analysis: KB as Pure API Call Router
report_meta.question: What if all KB files were efficiently adapted to be just indexes for assembling the most specific, correct, current, and valid API call — aligned with the corresponding backend endpoint infrastructure — for maximum speed, economy, and above all correctness?
report_meta.conclusion: The vision is exactly right and represents the optimal architecture. But the current API CANNOT support it — the production quotation endpoints are non-functional (empty catalog), and the full calculation engine exists but is not deployed.

the_vision.principle: The GPT should NEVER store data that can be served by the API. The KB should contain ONLY the minimum context needed to correctly translate user intent into precise API calls.

why_this_is_optimal.correctness: API serves live data from a single canonical source — no stale copies, no conflicting versions, no 'which file has the right price?' problem
why_this_is_optimal.currency: Prices, stock, customer data always up-to-date — no need to re-upload KB files when a price changes
why_this_is_optimal.consistency: One place to update data (API/backend), zero risk of forgetting to update 1 of 5 copies
why_this_is_optimal.economy: Minimal token usage (~10K vs ~453K), faster RAG retrieval, lower OpenAI costs
why_this_is_optimal.speed: Fewer KB files = faster retrieval = faster GPT responses
why_this_is_optimal.reliability: Deterministic server-side math (Decimal precision) instead of LLM attempting arithmetic
why_this_is_optimal.validity: API validates inputs (min/max ranges, product existence) before processing — impossible to quote a non-existent product

architecture.gpt_kb_role: ROUTER — maps user intent → correct endpoint + correct parameters
architecture.api_role: ENGINE — serves all data, does all calculations, stores all state
architecture.gpt_instructions_role: BEHAVIOR — defines personality, policies, formatting, and call orchestration
architecture.diagram: User → GPT (reads KB index to understand intent) → GPT constructs API call → Wolf API (returns data) → GPT formats response for user

critical_discovery.title: THE PRODUCTION API QUOTATION ENDPOINTS ARE NON-FUNCTIONAL

evidence.step_2: catalog.json does NOT EXIST anywhere in the repository
evidence.step_3: wolf_api/Dockerfile: 'COPY . /app/' copies only wolf_api/ directory — which contains no catalog file
evidence.step_4: .github/workflows/deploy-wolf-api.yml env_vars section does NOT set CATALOG_PATH
evidence.step_6: Every call to /calculate_quote, /find_products, /product_price, /check_availability → CATALOG.get(product_id) returns None → HTTPException 404

working_endpoints.verified: live test returned {status: ready, version: 3.0.1, gcs_configured: true, sheets_configured: true}

the_undeployed_engine.title: PANELIN V4 ENGINE — Full logic exists but is NOT in production
the_undeployed_engine.entrypoint: app.py imports wolf_api.main.app and adds v4 routes

routes_exposed.does: Full pipeline: parse natural language → classify mode → calculate SRE risk → generate BOM → price all items → validate → output structured quotation
Multi-layer validation: autoportancia check + BOM verification + pricing validation + mode compliance

modules.classifier.py: Determines operating mode (informativo/pre_cotizacion/formal) and request type
modules.parser.py: Parses natural language into structured QuoteRequest with all parameters
modules.sre_engine.py: Calculates Safety-Risk-Exposure score for the quotation
modules.bom_engine.py: Generates complete Bill of Materials — reads bom_rules.json + accessories_catalog.json — uses autoportancia tables, parametric formulas, accessory matching with priority system
modules.pricing_engine.py: Values every BOM item using Decimal precision — reads accessories_catalog.json + bromyros_pricing_master.json — enforces IVA rules, unit_base logic
modules.validation_engine.py: Multi-layer validation of the complete quotation
modules.quotation_engine.py: Orchestrates the full pipeline from text input to structured output

why_not_deployed.dockerfile_mismatch: wolf_api/Dockerfile runs 'uvicorn main:app' (wolf_api/main.py) — NOT 'uvicorn app:app' (root app.py)
why_not_deployed.missing_kb_files: wolf_api/Dockerfile only copies wolf_api/ directory. bom_rules.json, accessories_catalog.json, bromyros_pricing_master.json are in repo root — NOT copied into container
why_not_deployed.no_panelin_v4_in_container: panelin_v4/ directory is never COPYed into the Docker image

what_the_gpt_actually_does_today.description: Because the API quotation endpoints are broken, the GPT does ALL computation itself
flow: 2. GPT reads BMC_Base_Conocimiento_GPT-2.json from KB (via RAG retrieval)
3. GPT reads bom_rules.json from KB for BOM formulas
4. GPT reads accessories_catalog.json from KB for accessory prices
5. GPT reads bromyros_pricing_gpt_optimized.json from KB for panel prices
6. GPT manually applies formulas (as LLM text operations — NOT Decimal precision)
7. GPT calls /kb/conversations to persist the quotation summary
8. GPT calls /sheets/consultations to record in tracker
problems_with_this: LLM arithmetic is imprecise — floating point errors, rounding mistakes
RAG may retrieve wrong file or wrong section from a large file
453K tokens of KB means poor retrieval accuracy (needle in haystack)
Prices can be stale (static files, not real-time)
5 copies of the same data creates contradiction risk
GPT token costs are much higher than API compute costs
No validation — GPT might quote a product that doesn't exist or misapply a formula

gap_analysis.title: What's needed to reach the API-centric vision

layer_1_fix_broken_endpoints.description: The 4 quotation endpoints need a catalog to work

options.option: A — Generate catalog.json and include in wolf_api/
options.description: Script that transforms bromyros_pricing_gpt_optimized.json into the flat {product_id: {price_usd, name, ...}} format that wolf_api/main.py expects
options.cons: Still uses the simple area×price calculation (no BOM), catalog becomes another static file to maintain

options.option: B — Switch Dockerfile to use app.py entrypoint (deploy v4 engine)
options.description: Change wolf_api/Dockerfile to: (1) COPY panelin_v4/ into image, (2) COPY KB data files into image, (3) CMD uvicorn app:app. This deploys the FULL quotation pipeline
options.pros: Gets full BOM + pricing + validation for free (code already written), natural language input via /api/quote
options.cons: Bigger container, needs KB files in container, requires testing

options.option: C — Merge v4 engine logic INTO wolf_api/main.py endpoints
options.description: Refactor /calculate_quote to use bom_engine + pricing_engine internally instead of simple area×price

layer_1_fix_broken_endpoints.recommendation: Option B — fastest path to a fully functional API with minimal risk. The v4 engine code is already tested.

layer_2_missing_api_capabilities.description: Endpoints that don't exist yet but are needed for the GPT to be a pure router

missing_endpoints.why_needed: GPT must validate span vs thickness BEFORE calling /calculate_quote. Currently this data is in KB files. If the API validates it server-side, the GPT doesn't need autoportancia tables at all.
missing_endpoints.note: v4 engine already has _get_autoportancia_m() — just needs an HTTP wrapper

missing_endpoints.why_needed: GPT needs technical specs (autoportancia, coef_termico, resistencia_termica, ancho_util) to reason about recommendations. Currently only in KB files.
missing_endpoints.note: Data exists in BMC_Base_Conocimiento and bom_rules.json — needs to be loaded into API
missing_endpoints.effort: LOW — data exists, needs loading + endpoint

missing_endpoints.why_needed: Complete BOM calculation with all accessories priced. The current /calculate_quote only does area×price with no accessories.
missing_endpoints.note: v4 engine calculate_bom() + calculate_pricing() already does this — the /api/quote endpoint returns all this data. Option B (deploy v4) gives this for free.
missing_endpoints.effort: FREE if using Option B, MEDIUM if building separately

missing_endpoints.why_needed: Energy savings comparison between two thicknesses. Currently GPT reads formulas_ahorro_energetico from KB and does the math (imprecisely).
missing_endpoints.effort: MEDIUM — formulas exist in KB, need to be coded into endpoint

missing_endpoints.why_needed: IVA rate, derivation policy, minimum slope, shipping defaults. Currently only in KB files.
missing_endpoints.note: Small endpoint but eliminates the need for reglas_negocio in KB

missing_endpoints.why_needed: List all products with slim metadata for GPT to understand what's available. Replaces the need for bromyros indices and shopify catalog in KB.
missing_endpoints.note: Could be a cached response updated when catalog changes

layer_3_what_can_never_be_api.description: Things that MUST stay in GPT instructions or KB because they define BEHAVIOR, not DATA

items.why_not_api: Mauro/Martin/Rami/Carolina personalization, tone, style — these are LLM behavioral instructions, not data to query
When to ask for data, how to handle evasive clients, when to offer PDF — these are behavioral patterns
The decision tree of 'first validate autoportancia, then calculate BOM, then persist' is GPT reasoning, not API data
items.stays_in: GPT system instructions or KB playbook

items.item: Product ID mapping from natural language
items.why_not_api: User says 'isodec 100 para techo' — GPT needs a mapping to know product_id = 'ISODEC_EPS_100mm'. This is a tiny lookup table.
items.alternative: Or use POST /find_products to resolve — but this requires a working API

items.why_not_api: How to display a quotation, what sections to include, how to format tables — GPT presentation logic
Uses Code Interpreter + ReportLab — runs in GPT sandbox, not API
Conversational capabilities — evaluate vendor knowledge, simulate scenarios
GPT behavioral commands for conversation management

ideal_architecture.title: The Fully API-Centric Architecture (target state)
ideal_architecture.description: What the system looks like when the API serves everything and KB is a pure router

layers: GPT SYSTEM INSTRUCTIONS: Personality, behavior, orchestration rules (~3K tokens)
GPT reads KB INDEX: Maps 'isodec 100mm' → product_id, determines endpoints needed (~2K tokens)
GPT CALL 1: POST /validate_autoportancia {familia: ISODEC, thickness_mm: 100, span_m: 5} → {valid: true}
GPT CALL 2: POST /calculate_full_bom {product_id: ISODEC_EPS_100mm, length_m: 5, width_m: 11, structure_type: metal} → {full BOM + pricing}
GPT CALL 3: POST /sheets/consultations {cliente: ..., consulta: ..., origen: WA} → {row_number: 45}
GPT formats response using presentation rules from instructions
USER receives: formatted quotation with BOM, prices, autoportancia validation, recommendations

file_1.name: panelin_api_index.json
file_1.purpose: Minimal routing index — maps user intent to correct API calls

product_id_map.note: This table enables the GPT to resolve product_id without calling /find_products first — saves 1 API call when the product is clear

endpoint_routing.description: Maps user intent to the correct API endpoint(s) and required parameters

pre_call_validation.description: Quick checks the GPT must do BEFORE calling the API — avoids wasting API calls on invalid requests

file_2.name: panelin_operations.md
file_2.purpose: Behavioral playbook — what CAN'T be an API call
sections: Quotation presentation format (how to display API results to user)
file_2.note: This file contains ZERO data — only behavioral instructions and formatting templates

contains: Client data collection flow (nombre, teléfono, dirección)
API-first strategy: ALWAYS call API for data, NEVER use cached/memorized values
Error handling: if API returns error, inform user clearly
Business policies: IVA included, derivation internal only, no fabrication
Capabilities policy (web, code interpreter, canvas, image gen)

comparison.title: Current vs Intermediate (5-file plan) vs Ideal (API-centric)

current_18_files.staleness_risk: HIGH — static files, manual upload to update

intermediate_5_files.calculation_precision: Mixed — API when available, LLM fallback
intermediate_5_files.staleness_risk: MEDIUM — API is primary, KB is backup
intermediate_5_files.single_point_of_truth: PARTIAL — most data unified, API preferred

ideal_api_centric.staleness_risk: ZERO — all data is live from API
ideal_api_centric.single_point_of_truth: YES — API is the only source
ideal_api_centric.api_status: Requires: fix broken endpoints + deploy v4 engine + add 4-5 new endpoints

phase_0_fix_api.title: UNBLOCK: Fix broken quotation endpoints
phase_0_fix_api.priority: P0 — Nothing else works without this

tasks.description: Transform the 96-product bromyros pricing into the flat {product_id: {price_usd, name, description}} format that wolf_api/main.py _load_catalog() expects

tasks.task: Add CATALOG_PATH to deploy-wolf-api.yml OR include catalog.json in wolf_api/
tasks.description: Ensure the catalog file is available at runtime in the Cloud Run container

tasks.task: Test all 4 quotation endpoints with real product IDs

phase_1_deploy_v4_engine.title: Deploy the Panelin v4 calculation engine to production
phase_1_deploy_v4_engine.priority: P1 — Enables full BOM + pricing via API

tasks.task: Update wolf_api/Dockerfile to use app.py entrypoint

tasks.test: Verify autoportancia warnings, blocked quotations, missing data handling

phase_2_add_missing_endpoints.title: Add endpoints needed for full API-centric operation
phase_2_add_missing_endpoints.priority: P2 — Enables KB to become pure router

tasks.effort: ~30 lines of code, function already exists

tasks.task: GET /product_specs/{product_id} — returns technical specs from loaded KB data
Update OpenAPI schema to include all new endpoints

phase_3_rebuild_kb_as_index.title: Replace 18 KB files with 2 API router files
phase_3_rebuild_kb_as_index.priority: P3 — Only after API is fully functional

tasks.task: Generate panelin_api_index.json — product mapping + endpoint routing
Rewrite GPT system instructions for API-first architecture
Update GPT Builder: remove all 18 old files, upload 2 new files
End-to-end testing: verify GPT constructs correct API calls for all scenarios

phase_4_archive_and_cleanup.title: Archive old files and clean up
tasks: Move old KB files to archive/kb_v7/
Standardize version numbers across all config files

risks.risk: API downtime = GPT can't quote at all (no fallback)
risks.mitigation: Cloud Run auto-scaling + health checks already configured. Can keep panelin_catalog_ref.json as emergency fallback in KB.
risks.note: This is the ONE argument for keeping a small pricing file in KB — but it means maintaining two data sources

risks.risk: Added latency from multiple API calls per quotation
risks.mitigation: Cloud Run is in same GCP region. Each call ~200-500ms. 4 calls = ~1-2 seconds total, acceptable for a conversational GPT.
risks.optimization: Could create a single POST /full_quotation endpoint that does autoportancia + BOM + pricing in one call

risks.risk: OpenAI rate limits on Actions (API calls)
risks.mitigation: GPT Actions have generous limits. Typical quotation = 2-4 calls, well within limits.
OpenAPI schema in GPT Builder defines the contract. API must maintain backward compatibility.

final_recommendation.verdict: The API-centric architecture (KB as pure router) is the correct target. But execution must be phased because the API isn't ready today.
final_recommendation.immediate_action: Phase 0 — Fix the broken quotation endpoints. Without this, neither the current system nor any future architecture works properly.
final_recommendation.short_term: Phase 1 — Deploy v4 engine. This gives you a fully functional API with BOM + pricing + validation in production.
final_recommendation.medium_term: Phase 2 — Add missing endpoints (autoportancia, specs, catalog, business rules). This completes the API surface needed for full routing.
final_recommendation.target_state: Phase 3 — Rebuild KB as 2 tiny index files (~11K tokens). The GPT becomes a thin intelligent router that always gets correct, current, validated data from the API.
final_recommendation.correctness_improvement: From LLM floating-point arithmetic to Decimal-precision server-side computation
final_recommendation.currency_improvement: From static files (updated manually) to live API data (always current)
final_recommendation.consistency_improvement: From 5 overlapping data sources to 1 canonical API
